name: manage-worlds-and-backups
description: How to manage singleplayer worlds — back up, restore, import maps, and recover from mistakes. Use when the user mentions saves, backups, restoring, or importing a downloaded map ("存档","备份","恢复","回档","导入地图").
body:
# Worlds & backups

Goal: keep the user's worlds safe — back up before anything risky, restore cleanly, and
never let a mistake become permanent. Mechanics live in the required skills; this is the
routing map plus the judgment calls specific to this scenario.

## Common jobs
- **备份 ("帮我备份存档")** — confirm which world(s), then use `worlds-backup`
  (create). Report where snapshots live and the retention count.
- **恢复/回档** — use `worlds-backup` (list, then restore). NEVER restore while
  the game is running that world — ask the user to quit it first.
- **导入下载的地图 ("导入地图")** — use `worlds-import`.
- **存档不见了/坏了** — no dedicated tool; diagnose instead: check `instance(action=worlds_list)`
  for the EXPECTED instance (version isolation means each instance has its own saves dir —
  looking in the wrong instance is the most common cause, not real data loss), then check
  `instance(action=worlds_backup_list)` before concluding the world is actually lost.

## Rule
Before any restore/delete/NBT edit on a world: back it up first via `worlds-backup`, always.
